title: Primula veris elatior
taxonomy: Primula veris elatior is classified as a subspecies of Primula veris, a species of flowering plant in the primrose family (Primulaceae). The genus Primula is part of the larger order Ericales in the angiosperm clade. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Ericales order. The Primula veris elatior subspecies is characterized by its distinct morphology and growth habits, which set it apart from other Primula veris subspecies. As a member of the Primulaceae family, Primula veris elatior shares traits such as simple, alternate leaves and showy, bell-shaped flowers.
uses: Primula veris elatior, also known as the greater cowslip, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems. In folk medicine, greater cowslip is believed to have a calming effect and has been used to treat anxiety and insomnia. However, its effectiveness for these purposes has not been extensively scientifically proven. More research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Primula veris elatior.

properties: Primula veris elatior, also known as the greater cowslip, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its therapeutic properties. intro: Primula veris elatior, also known as the ox-eyed primrose, is a flowering plant species within the primrose family (Primulaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, coughs, and sore throats, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Primula veris elatior is rich in bioactive constituents, including iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory complaints and as a natural expectorant.
